# Understanding passive voice in API documentation — writing passive.md

This article explores when passive voice is used and when to avoid it.

## Why passive voice is used in tech docs

Passive voice is often used in technical writing. Results are returned by the server. Errors are handled by the middleware. Requests are processed asynchronously.

## Configuration

Configuration can be updated by editing the settings file. Once saved, the changes are applied automatically by the system. If an error is encountered, the application is restarted by the process manager.

Mistakes were made in the previous release. Assumptions were made about the default behavior that turned out to be incorrect. A decision was made to deprecate the old API endpoint without notifying downstream consumers.

## Deployment

The deployment was triggered last Thursday. A build failure was observed by the on-call team. The incident was escalated and the root cause was identified. A fix was deployed and the service was restored.

It should be noted that the rollback process was complicated by the fact that migrations had already been applied. It is recommended that you back up your database before deploying schema changes.

## Access control

Access to the production environment is restricted. Permissions are granted on a need-to-know basis. Requests for elevated access should be submitted through the access request portal, where they will be reviewed.
